items.title: Umno assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan dismiss rumours of U‑turn, await Zahid Hamidi’s instructions
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 29 — Negeri Sembilan Umno leaders have dismissed speculation that several of their assemblymen may return to support the state Pakatan Harapan government, insisting they will act only on instructions from party president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Star reported that state Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias rejected claims that four assemblymen were preparing to reverse their decision and back Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. “When the 14 of us made a decision to declare a loss of confidence in Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s leadership on Monday, it was made en bloc.

items.title: Mercedes quarterly profit slumps as China, tariffs weigh
items.summary: The automaker reported earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) of 1.9 billion euros (US$2.22 billion), down 17 per cent, but higher than the average analyst estimate of 1.6 billion euros, according to a poll conducted by Visible Alpha. The Stuttgart-based company posted first-quarter revenue of 31.6 billion euros, missing analyst estimates for 31.8 billion euros. The adjusted return on sales for Mercedes' core cars division was 4.1 per cent, within a full-year target of between 3 per cent and 5 per cent, but down from 7.3 per cent in the same quarter last year.

items.title: Ex-Bersatu leader's shooting: Police record nine statements
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have recorded statements from nine witnesses following an incident in which former Johor Bersatu associate wing chief Datuk Tan Lek Khang accidentally shot himself at the Rem Estate shooting range in Kota Tinggi yesterday. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the witnesses included the 40-year-old victim's 19-year-old stepson, a coach, five bodyguards and two friends. Based on witness accounts, the incident is believed to have occurred while the victim was clearing his firearm after completing a training session, he told a press conference at the Johor police headquarters here today.

items.title: Diesel wholesaler fined RM130,000 for false invoices, withholding information
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, 29 April — A company involved in retail fuel sales was fined a total of RM130,000 by the Sessions Court today on four counts of providing false information on invoices and refusing to submit information relating to its diesel purchases. Judge Nor Azah Kasran meted out the fine after the company, Profusion Petroleum Sdn Bhd, represented by its director, Lai Yong Keong, 47, pleaded guilty to all the charges. The false information was related to invoices or receipts for diesel purchases involving nine invoices.

items.title: Foreigners attempt balcony escape in Immigration raid at Pantai Dalam flats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In a desperate bid to evade arrest, several foreigners risked their safety by scaling down balconies between units during an Immigration Department raid at a five-storey flat in Pantai Dalam. A Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department spokesperson said a total of 90 individuals were screened, comprising 74 men and 16 women of various nationalities, including locals. He said 46 individuals were detained, comprising 43 men and three women, including 29 Pakistani men, nine Indonesian men, seven Indonesian women and one Indian man.

items.title: A-GC rejects Namewee's bid to drop drug, poison possession charges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Controversial director and rapper, Namewee, has failed in his bid to have his drug-related charges dropped or reduced. Deputy public prosecutor Nurainizatul Farhana Zainal informed magistrate Atiqah Mohamed @ Mohamad Saim that the Attorney-General's Chambers had rejected the representation submitted by Namewee. Namewee, whose real name is Wee Meng Chee, 43, had previously filed a representation seeking a review of charges for possessing prohibited substances, namely sildenafil and methamphetamine.

items.title: Sabah nears full digital coverage, reaches 98.7 per cent connectivity in populated areas
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah has achieved 98.7 per cent internet and communications coverage in populated areas as of Feb 28, 2026, marking a significant improvement from 73.41 per cent before the rollout of the National Digital Network Plan, or JENDELA. Sabah Minister of Education, Science, Technology and Innovation Datuk James Ratib said the progress reflects the federal government's continued commitment, together with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), to strengthening digital infrastructure across the country, particularly in Sabah. Meanwhile, Sabah Assistant Minister of Works and Utilities Ruddy Awah said the state government is upgrading sewerage pipelines in Kota Kinabalu using Cured-In-Place Pipe (CIPP) technology.

items.title: Teacher acquitted of sexual assault charges involving 15-year-old student
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 32-year-old teacher was acquitted today of two charges of sexually assaulting her 15-year-old male student in a car and at a bus stop three years ago. The charge was brought under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping. The second charge was framed under Section 14(b) of the same act, read with Section 16(1).

items.title: Indonesia military court begins trial of officers allegedly behind acid attack on activist
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesian military prosecutors charged four officers on Wednesday for their alleged involvement in an acid attack on an activist known for campaigning against the expanding public role of the armed forces, seeking a maximum sentence of 12 years in prison. The attack, which left Andrie permanently scarred and his right eye heavily damaged, drew local and international condemnation. Mohammad Iswadi, a military prosecutor, said during the hearing at a military court that the four officers from the military's intelligence unit allegedly attacked Andrie because they felt affronted by his protest last year against legal changes allowing more military officers to be appointed to civilian government posts.

items.title: Gamuda's JV scoops RM5.98bil water contract in Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gamuda Bhd's joint venture has secured a RM5.98 billion contract for Phase 1 of the Skim Bekalan Air Ulu Padas (SBAUP) project in Sabah. The construction giant said in a Bursa Malaysia filing that Upper Padas Power Sdn Bhd had received a letter of acceptance from the Sabah State Water Department on April 29 for the construction of Phase 1 of the project. Gamuda said Phase 1 involves the construction of a 350 million litres per day water treatment plant in Beaufort, along with about 200km of pipelines across Sabah's west coast from Sipitang to Kota Kinabalu.

items.title: Man gets 18 years' jail, two strokes for causing death of adopted son
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A lorry attendant was sentenced to 18 years' imprisonment and two strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to causing the death of his three-year-old adopted son on April 11. The accused, Muhammad Firdaus Zamri, 30, entered the plea after two charges were read before Sessions Court judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli. For the first charge, the court sentenced him to 18 years' imprisonment, while for the second charge he was given four years' jail and two strokes of the rotan.

items.title: Chief Statistician urges Malaysians to rebuild discipline, warning QR payments make saving harder than ever
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — From stamp books and post office savings to instant QR payments, Malaysians’ relationship with money has shifted dramatically — and not necessarily for the better. That is the candid view of Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin, who said the convenience of digital payments may be quietly eroding the discipline that once defined how Malaysians saved. The underlying principle, he said, remains unchanged — saving is essentially about discipline.

items.title: Trump to put his picture in US passports
items.summary: WASHINGTON: An image of Donald Trump will soon appear in some US passports, officials said Tuesday, shattering another norm as the president aggressively puts his personal stamp on government institutions. The State Department said it would offer the limited-edition passport to mark this year's 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence. Trump's signature — in gold — lies underneath.

items.title: Australia's ESafety chief says she faced death threats after social media ban
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia's top online safety regulator has revealed that she received death threats and widespread abuse following the rollout of a controversial social media ban, Anadolu Ajansi reported, citing SBS News on Wednesday. Julie Inman Grant, who heads the country's eSafety Commission, said the backlash intensified after new rules delaying children's access to social media until the age of 16 were announced. she said.

items.title: Stats Dept warns Malaysians: Surplus years limited to ages 29–55, save wisely before income declines
items.summary: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the data painted a stark but familiar picture of the country’s financial lifecycle — one where income only meaningfully exceeds spending between the ages of 29 and 55. The NTA framework tracks how labour income compares with consumption across a person’s life, revealing two prolonged deficit phases — at the beginning and end of life — sandwiching a relatively short surplus period in the middle. In the early years, from birth up to age 28, Malaysians operate in a “life cycle deficit”, where spending outpaces income.

items.title: Transport company supervisor killed after being run over
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A transport company supervisor was killed after he was run over by a vehicle at Jalan Pelabuhan Barat in Klang on Tuesday night. Klang Selatan police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said the 37-year-old victim was riding towards Shah Alam at 9.45pm when he lost control of his motorcycle. The victim crashed and was subsequently run over by an unidentified vehicle.

items.title: Man arrested for allegedly abusing 5-month-old son
items.summary: LAWAS: Police have arrested a 28-year-old man for allegedly abusing his 5-month-old son, who was found with multiple bruises while receiving treatment at Lawas Hospital on Sunday. The case came to light when a medical officer at the hospital lodged a report after suspecting the infant's injuries were not accidental. District police chief Deputy Superintendent Liong Tindan said the infant was brought to the hospital by his mother at 10.45pm for a cold and cough.

items.title: Malaysia's inflation eases to 1.4pct in 2025: DOSM
items.summary: Chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the country's inflation in 2025 was contributed by the inflation of housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels (1.6 per cent); health (1.2 per cent); recreation, sports and culture (1.1 per cent); transport (0.4 per cent) and furnishings, household equipment and routine household maintenance (0.2 per cent). He said personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services recorded a higher increase at 4.4 per cent compared to 2024's 3.0 per cent, followed by insurance and financial services (3.4 per cent), restaurants and accommodation services (3.2 per cent), education (2.3 per cent), and food and beverages (2.1 per cent). Meanwhile, he said most states registered slower inflation, with four states exceeding the national inflation rate of 1.4 per cent in 2025, namely Johor (2.0 per cent), Selangor (1.7 per cent), Negeri Sembilan (1.7 per cent) and Melaka (1.5 per cent).

items.title: Experimental cafe run by AI opens in Stockholm
items.summary: THE avocado toasts and baristas making foamy lattes make it look like any other cafe, except at this one, located in a Stockholm residential neighbourhood, artificial intelligence (AI) is running the place. Behind the counter is barista Kajetan Grzelczak who was hired by "Mona", the AI cafe manager – which is powered by Google's Gemini. Grzelczak told AFP that "ordering isn't really her best suit."

items.title: Unlicensed tour operators face fines and jail, Sabah official says
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Tourists and operators found conducting unlicensed tourism activities in Sabah will face enforcement action, including fines, jail terms or deportation for foreigners. Assistant Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Dr Andi Md Shamsureezal Mohd Sainal said such offences fall under Act 482 and are enforced by the federal government through the Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (Motac). Action may also be taken under other relevant laws by agencies such as the Immigration Department, local authorities and other relevant bodies.

items.title: Search continues for boy swept into 3m drain in Puncak Alam
items.summary: PUNCAK ALAM: A search and rescue operation is ongoing for a young boy who was swept away by strong currents after falling into a drain about 3m deep. "The drainage here is uncovered and deep. The search and rescue operation is ongoing.

items.title: Crackdown on illegal tourism turned up 71 cases of unlicensed operators in Sabah since last year
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Authorities have opened dozens of investigation papers against illegal tourism operators in Sabah since last year as part of an ongoing crackdown on unlicensed activities in the sector, the state assembly was told today. Assistant Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Dr Andi Md Shamsureezal said a total of 47 investigation papers were opened in 2025, with compounds amounting to RM290,000. Dr Andi said enforcement against unlicensed tourism operators falls under the Tourism Industry Act 1992 (Act 482), which is under the jurisdiction of the federal Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture.

items.title: Selangor to encourage data centres to use raw or recycled water to not affect consumer water supply
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 29 — New data centre developments in Selangor are encouraged to use recycled water or treat raw water to suitable standards for cooling system needs. "Water demand from data centres is indeed very high. However, we must distinguish between water required for data centres and drinking water.

items.title: Singapore Open: Ham overcomes rain delay to seal maiden international triumph
items.summary: SINGAPORE: South Korean golfer Ham Jeong-woo, 31, ranked world No. At Sentosa Golf Club's Serapong course, Ham held off a strong challenge from Australia's Cameron John, who finished two strokes behind. With the victory, Ham claimed the lion's share of the US$2 million (RM9.5 million) prize purse, taking home the winner's cheque of US$360,000 (RM1.7 million).

items.title: Judiciary reflects merit, not gender, chief justice says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's judiciary has seen women hold all four top posts, proving talent knows no gender, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh said on Wednesday. Wan Ahmad Farid said the achievements were proof that justice does not falter in the hands of women but leads. The top judge said justice must extend beyond courtrooms into institutional culture, leadership and the daily experience of professionals.

items.title: Drone diplomacy wins Ukraine valuable allies, but now it must deliver
items.summary: PRESIDENT Volodymyr Zelenskyy has leveraged Ukraine's expertise in drone warfare into a series of successful diplomatic deals during visits to the Middle East and Europe, showcasing how Kyiv is using military prowess to boost its diplomatic clout. Ukraine has also developed long-range attack drone capabilities to hit Russian energy infrastructure. "Ukraine now needs to organise itself to actually deliver."

items.title: Universities can lead fight against digital colonisation
items.summary: LETTERS: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is often associated with Silicon Valley and Europe while countries in the Global South are seen as mere recipients of technology. China, however, has demonstrated that AI development can be driven by a strong domestic ecosystem, with universities and technology companies collaborating to build language models, intelligent surveillance systems, and large scale industrial applications. Universities must serve as fertile ground for AI ecosystems rooted in local culture and language, ensuring that models developed do not simply reproduce Western narratives but generate new signs that reflect Malaysia's identity.

items.title: Flight cancellations and high fuel prices help keep Chinese close to home for May holidays
items.summary: BEIJING/HONG KONG: Travel demand in China is expected to remain strong during the five-day Labour Day holiday from May 1, with travellers opting for domestic road trips and independent tours as higher fuel costs curb demand for overseas holidays. The price rises have triggered a wave of flight cancellations on routes between China and Southeast Asia. Multiple carriers — including Air China, China Eastern, Spring Airlines and Malaysia-based AirAsia — have reduced or suspended services between China and popular destinations such as Bangkok, Phuket and Kuala Lumpur, according to domestic media.

items.title: Man captured, put on seven day remand after being on the run for allegedly beating wife so severely she miscarried
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, April 29 — A man accused of assaulting his wife so severely that she suffered a miscarriage in Ambangan Height last Thursday has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations. Police arrested the suspect yesterday in Alor Setar after he fled following the incident. At the time, Johor Bahru police said they were seeking a witness to assist investigations under Section 325 of the Penal Code.

items.title: RTS Link to reshape Johor Baru's commercial landscape, calls mount for coordinated action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link is poised to reshape Johor Baru's commercial landscape, with policymakers and industry leaders calling for urgent, coordinated action to ensure the city is ready to capture its full economic potential. Speaking at the Cerebrum Insights Workshop: RTS 2026 & Johor CBD Opportunities, Johor Corp (JCorp) president and chief executive Datuk Syed Mohamed Syed Ibrahim described the RTS as a "once-in-a-generation commercial catalyst" that will inject sustained footfall into the city. Syed Mohamed challenged the stakeholders to ensure the city is prepared.

items.title: Censuria, Affin Group align on family office strategy ahead of SFO scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Censuria Family Office has engaged Affin Group's wealth management and financial advisory arm to develop its family office strategy under the Affin Diventium Private Banking segment. Datuk Marco Yap who heads Censuria Family Office said the group has earned its confidence with its comprehensive investment banking, wealth management, and brokerage solutions. " "Together, we aim to drive long-term portfolio growth, co-investment initiatives, and strategic wealth management outcomes, while strengthening Malaysia's family office ecosystem."

items.title: Remand extended for army officer suspected of drunk driving in fatal MEX crash
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 29 — The remand period for the army officer suspected of driving under the influence in a fatal crash along the Maju Expressway (MEX) here has been extended until tomorrow. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the extension was necessary to allow police to complete their investigation papers, according to Berita Harian . “The investigation papers are not yet complete and will be referred tomorrow morning for further instructions.

items.title: Driver narrowly avoids falling tree on storm-hit road in Seremban [WATCH]
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A driver narrowly escaped injury after a large tree toppled across a road in Taman Bukit Galena here during torrential rain and strong winds. The 20-second TikTok clip showed a vehicle travelling along a road in heavy rain, with visibility reduced. The driver immediately brakes, just avoiding being hit by the falling tree.

items.title: Malaysia weighs new limits on youngsters' access to social media
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is preparing to introduce new rules restricting independent social media access for those under 16 as part of a broader push to improve online safety for children. The policy, expected to take effect in the middle of this year, will require social media platforms to prevent anyone under 16 from managing accounts independently. Malaysia will not impose a full ban on social media access.

items.title: Man duped by Facebook investment scam promising returns of up to 750pc, loses RM610,000
items.summary: MUAR, April 29 — A businessman lost RM610,000 after falling victim to a non-existent investment scheme that promised unusually high returns. Muar district police chief ACP Raiz Mukhliz Azman Aziz said the 49-year-old man was drawn to an investment advertisement on Facebook before getting in touch with the suspect via WhatsApp. Raiz Mukhliz said the victim was also informed by the suspect that all investment matters would be handled by a broker.

items.title: Taiwan's TSMC exits Arm with US$231mil share sale
items.summary: Its subsidiary, TSMC Partners, sold 1.11 million Arm shares from April 28 to 29 at US$207.65 each, for a total of about US$231 million, the filing showed. The disposal resulted in an impact on retained earnings of US$174 million. Following the transaction, TSMC no longer holds any Arm shares.

items.title: Sinopec refineries cut Q1 production, LNG import business incurs US$121mil loss
items.summary: BEIJING: China's Sinopec Corp said on Wednesday its refineries cut production by around 7.6 percentage points on an annualised basis in the first quarter of 2026, with utilisation rates averaging around 83 per cent. Formerly known as China Petroleum & Chemical Corp, Sinopec's ethylene utilisation rate stood at 89 per cent in the first quarter, 1.5 percentage points lower year on year, a company executive said. Sinopec's first-quarter liquefied natural gas import business incurred an 830 million yuan (US$121.46 million) loss due to reduced supplies under term supplies and higher spot imports, the executive said.

items.title: Palm tracks Dalian palm olein, Chicago soyoil lower
items.summary: JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures fell on Wednesday, tracking weakness in Dalian palm olein and Chicago soyoil. The benchmark palm oil contract for July delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid RM32, or 0.71 per cent, to RM4,504 (US$1,140.25) a metric ton in early trade. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract rose 0.16 per cent, while its palm oil contract shed 0.62 per cent.

items.title: Stocks retreat on Iran, AI concerns as Fed meeting looms
items.summary: S&P 500 e-mini futures nudged up 0.1 per cent, while Brent crude rose 0.4 per cent to US$111.71 per barrel as efforts to end the Iran conflict hit an impasse. US President Donald Trump is unhappy with the latest proposal from Tehran as he wants nuclear issues dealt with from the outset, a US official said. The Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday, citing US officials, that Trump has instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran.

items.title: Police probe RM415,000 Haj permit scam after Kuching man tricked by suspect in Mecca
items.summary: Kuching police chief ACP Alexson Naga Chabu said the victim was introduced to the Malay male suspect on October 30, 2024, at a restaurant near The Clock Towers in Mecca. The victim was offered assistance to obtain Haj permits and to invest in accommodation for pilgrims during the Haj and Umrah seasons. “Attracted by the offer, the victim made six online payment transactions to two different bank accounts.

items.title: Drop ‘negeri’ label, Jeffrey Kitingan insists, as Sabah deserves territory status
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Tambunan Assemblyman Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Kitingan called on the Government to drop the term “negeri” for Sabah, saying Sabah is a founding partner of Malaysia and not on the same level as the peninsular states. “The Sabah Legislative Assembly has already begun this shift, we now call it the Dewan Undangan Sabah, not Dewan Undangan Negeri and Sabah elections are now referred to as Pilihan Raya Umum Dewan Undangan Sabah, not Pilihan Raya Negeri,” he said. Sabah must stand as an equal to Sarawak and Malaya.

items.title: Cable theft suspects injured after 4WD crashes while fleeing police in Tuaran
items.summary: Tuaran Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) Chief Kurong Ak Beki said the department received a call about the incident at 4.44am. “Upon arrival, firefighters found the accident involved two vehicles — a Toyota Hilux Vigo carrying four victims (suspects) and an Isuzu Invader with one victim. Kurong said firefighters extricated the elderly man who was trapped in the Isuzu Invader using special equipment.

items.title: Three teenage boys arrested in Tawau over alleged sexual crimes against 14‑year‑old girl
items.summary: TAWAU, April 29 — Three teenage boys were arrested for alleged sexual crimes against a 14-year-old girl in several incidents since late last year. Tawau District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said two of the suspects, aged 14 and 17, were arrested in the Kg Jawa area at 11.50am on Monday by a team from the District Criminal Investigation Division (BSJD). “The arrests were made after police received a report last Saturday regarding allegations of sexual offences committed against the alleged victim at several separate locations around here between November 2025 and February this year,” he said.

items.title: Sabah assemblyman demands crackdown on foreigners fronting shops under locals’ names
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Usukan assemblyman Isnaraissah Munirah Majilis wants action taken on foreigners running businesses using Sabahans’ names and cited Pakistani-manned sundry shops statewide that are killing local businesses. It is an issue of local economic sovereignty,” she said when debating the Head of State Policy Speech, Tuesday. They are Pakistani,” she said, calling on the public to support only local-run businesses.

items.title: King of Malaysia meets Johor Menteri Besar at Istana Pasir Pelangi to discuss current issues
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 29 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Menteri Besar Johor Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi. During the meeting, His Majesty was briefed on matters relating to state development, the well-being of the people and current issues. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, the audience session took place at Istana Pasir Pelangi, here.

items.title: James Chai back at MACC for second day of testimony in RM1.1b semiconductor probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 29 — A former aide to Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, Chai Jin Shern or James Chai, is back at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters here today to continue providing his statement in connection with an ongoing investigation. On the second day of the investigation, Chai arrived at 9.45 am to assist in the probe involving Arm Holdings Plc, a leading semiconductor company based in the United Kingdom. The anti-graft agency opened an investigation paper on Feb 16 into an agreement between the Malaysian government and a foreign company, reportedly valued at RM1.1 billion.

items.title: Comprehensive documentation key to preserving integrity in crisis, says EAIC chairman amid rising supply strains
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 29 — Systematic documentation of decisions is a critical safeguard in maintaining the integrity of enforcement agencies as the country copes with the global supply crisis arising from conflicts in West Asia, according to Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) chairman Tan Sri Dr Ismail Bakar. He said that in a crisis requiring swift action and involving significant public spending, every enforcement decision must be properly recorded, reported transparently and subject to audit to prevent abuse of power and sustain public trust. “In times of crisis, comprehensive documentation is not merely an administrative requirement, but the foundation of integrity and accountability in every enforcement action.

items.title: PAS sets sights on 16 Melaka seats as state election gears up for December showdown
items.summary: MELAKA, April 29 — PAS is set to contest 16 of the 28 seats in the upcoming Melaka state election. Melaka PAS Commissioner Zulkifli Ismail said the party has identified the following constituencies — Pantai Kundor, Paya Rumput, Klebang, Tanjung Bidara, Ayer Limau, Linggi, Taboh Naning, Rembia, Durian Tunggal, Ayer Molek, Pengkalan Batu, Telok Mas, Duyong, Sungai Rambai, Merlimau and Serkam. “This decision is in line with the announcement made by PAS Central Election Director Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor during the recent launch of Melaka PAS’ election machinery,” he said in a statement posted on the Melaka PAS Facebook page today.

items.title: MetMalaysia flags Level 1 hot weather alert across 17 areas nationwide as mercury climbs past 35°C
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — A total of 14 areas in Peninsular Malaysia, two in Sabah and one in Sarawak recorded hot weather at Level 1, or the alert category, as of 6am today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said in a statement that the affected areas were Perlis; Kubang Pasu, Pendang, Kota Setar and Padang Terap in Kedah; Kuala Kangsar and Hulu Perak in Perak; Jeli, Pasir Mas, Tanah Merah and Machang in Kelantan; Kuala Lumpur; as well as Gombak and Sepang in Selangor. Also affected were Beaufort and Telupid in Sabah, and Limbang in Sarawak.

items.title: Another Sandakan blaze leaves 30 families displaced, coming less than two weeks after 1,000‑home inferno
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 29 — About 30 non-permanent homes spanning 3.5 acres were destroyed in an early morning fire at a village behind PPH along Jalan Bokara, Karamunting. Fire crews used water from fire engines and nearby hydrants, and successfully prevented the flames from spreading to around 100 nearby homes. The incident occurred just 11 days after a separate fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Kampung Bahagia, roughly 10km away in the same area.

items.title: Pontian police confirm arrest of school van driver seen driving against traffic in viral video
items.summary: PONTIAN, April 29 — Police have confirmed that the school van driver featured in a viral video showing the vehicle being driven against traffic has been detained for further investigation. According to Harian Metro , Pontian district police chief Superintendent Hadzrat Hussien Mion Hussain said the 64-year-old man was taken into custody when he turned up at the Pontian District Police Headquarters (IPD) to have his statement recorded. Commenting further, Hadzrat said police detected the viral footage on the Facebook page “Geng Pontian” at 7.55am yesterday.

items.title: High Court blocks RM6.7m transactions in Barbados as MACC widens 1MDB asset hunt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has obtained a prohibitory order on transactions involving funds amounting to USD1.7 million (RM6.7 million) held in Barbados, believed to be linked to funds misappropriated from 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB). MACC, in a statement yesterday, said the funds were held in two bank accounts at Amicorp Bank and Trust Limited in Barbados. “The application showed that the funds are linked to money laundering activities based on investigations by MACC’s Special Operations Division,” it said.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu says rice stockpile hits 300,000 tonnes as Malaysia braces for rising feed and fuel costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — The country’s food supply remains stable for now, with the rice buffer stock reaching 300,000 tonnes, says Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu. He said the local production of chicken and eggs was also close to achieving full self-sufficiency. Although the current situation was under control, Mohamad said the government remained cautious of challenges arising from rising costs of animal feed, which still relied on imports, as well as fuel prices.

items.title: James Chai set to return to MACC today with investigators chasing leads in RM1.1b saga
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 29 — Former aide to Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, Chai Jin Shern, known as James Chai, will return to give evidence at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today. MACC took a statement from political analyst Chai, a witness in the probe into a government-funded semiconductor project worth roughly RM1.1 billion. Sources said the witness showed up at MACC headquarters in Putrajaya to share information and aid the investigation.

items.title: How cheap can public hospitals get? Here's a how-to guide to receiving care from MOH specialised institutions
items.summary: Hospital Permai, Johor Bahru: A dedicated psychiatric and mental health hospital. Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak: Specialises in psychiatric care. Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang, Sabah: Another dedicated psychiatric hospital.

items.title: Owner claims 100-year legacy wiped out as Penang demolishes Waterfall Cafe
items.summary: S. Ananth Raj said the restaurant was first started by his grandfather within the Penang Botanic Gardens during the British colonial era, where rent was paid to the authorities at the time. “Later, we were relocated outside the Botanic Gardens, to the roundabout area that has since been built there,” he said in a statement. Waterfall Cafe owner S. Ananth Raj said the demolition has destroyed the family’s livelihood.

items.title: Overcrowded, understaffed and in urgent need of upgrades: Sabah lawmaker says Sandakan Hospital buckling under strain
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 28 — Healthcare services in Sandakan are struggling to keep pace with demand, making it the hospital with some of the highest mortality rates in the country, Elopura assemblyman Calvin Chong said in the Sabah Legislative Assembly today. “Patient overcrowding, insufficient wards, lack of parking and staff, lack of health workers and specialists, and hospital facilities are getting worse. “There should be a clear timeline to make Sandakan a health hub in Sabah,” he said.

items.title: Pakatan condemns ‘betrayal’ by 14 Negeri Sembilan Umno lawmakers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR April 28 Pakatan Harapan (PH) has strongly condemned the actions of 14 Umno state assemblymen from the Barisan Nasional (BN) coalition in Negeri Sembilan, accusing them of betraying the spirit of the Unity Government and attempting to destabilise the state administration. PH rejected what it termed as the “betrayal” by the 14 UMNO-BN lawmakers who allegedly attempted to “topple the roof” and seize control of the Negeri Sembilan state government administration. Despite the political manoeuvring, PH reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining stability in the state.

items.title: Former Rafizi aide James Chai seen leaving MACC at 6.30pm, more than eight hours after arriving to give statement
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 28 — Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli's former aide Chai Jin Shern, also known as James Chai, has finished giving his statement to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today in connection with an investigation involving a leading global semiconductor firm. Earlier, James Chai arrived at the MACC Headquarters at 10 am with his lawyer. He, however, refused to be interviewed by media practitioners gathered at the MACC Headquarters.

items.title: Poultry up slightly, beef cheaper but overall food prices remain controlled, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Selected food prices in Malaysia remain broadly stable, with cost-of-living pressures still contained despite early signs of rising input costs, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. He said the average price of standard chicken rose by 3.3 per cent to RM9.70 per kilogramme, while Grade C chicken eggs increased by 7.3 per cent to RM3.93 per 10 pieces. However, he said beef prices declined by 3.8 per cent to RM35.92 per kilogramme.
